title: Bronchial Asthma
root_mechanism: Atopic sensitisation + environmental triggers → mast cell degranulation → Th2-mediated eosinophilic bronchial inflammation → bronchial smooth muscle hyperreactivity + mucus hypersecretion + subepithelial fibrosis → airflow obstruction.

interventions.intervention: Nasal irrigation (Jala Neti with saline) + warm steam inhalation with eucalyptus
interventions.physiological_effect: Nasal irrigation clears allergens and reduces nasal eosinophil load; eucalyptus 1,8-cineole is mucolytic and bronchodilatory

interventions.intervention: Buteyko breathing exercise (nasal-only, reduced volume breathing)
interventions.physiological_effect: Raises CO2 → smooth muscle relaxation via Bohr effect → bronchodilation; breaks hyperventilation-bronchospasm cycle

interventions.intervention: Pranayama: Anulom Vilom + slow Kapalabhati (30 strokes/min only)
interventions.physiological_effect: Slow Kapalabhati increases alveolar ventilation without bronchospasm; Anulom Vilom reduces mast cell reactivity via vagal modulation

interventions.intervention: Anti-inflammatory, anti-allergic: turmeric milk, vitamin C fruits, honey + ginger
interventions.physiological_effect: Quercetin stabilises mast cells; vitamin C reduces histamine levels; ginger inhibits 5-LOX and prostaglandin E2

interventions.intervention: Chest mustard oil pack (warm) + percussion massage on upper back
interventions.physiological_effect: Mustard oil allyl isothiocyanate increases bronchial blood flow; percussion vibrates bronchial walls → loosens mucus plugs

interventions.intervention: Vasaka (Malabar nut) + Licorice (Yashtimadhu) decoction
interventions.physiological_effect: Vasaka alkaloid vasicine is potent bronchodilator and mucolytic (10× bromhexine); licorice glycyrrhizin inhibits 11β-HSD → anti-inflammatory

interventions.intervention: Anti-histamine, anti-inflammatory: no dairy, rainbow vegetables + fish
interventions.physiological_effect: Dairy increases mucus viscosity; omega-3 shifts LTB4 to LTB5 → weaker bronchospasm mediator

interventions.intervention: Slow 20-min nasal-only walk in pollution-free environment
interventions.physiological_effect: Nasal breathing warms, humidifies and filters air → reduces exercise-induced bronchospasm; aerobic exercise improves FEV1

interventions.intervention: Cold water chest pack (upper chest/sternum)
interventions.physiological_effect: Cold → reflex vasodilation on rewarming → improved bronchial mucosal circulation; reduces eosinophilic infiltration

interventions.intervention: Yoga sequence — NO Kapalabhati in active wheeze (see below)
interventions.physiological_effect: Chest-opening postures increase FRC; diaphragm strengthening reduces accessory muscle use; inversions drain sinus congestion

interventions.intervention: Steam inhalation with ajwain (carom seeds) + turmeric
interventions.physiological_effect: Ajwain thymol is potent bronchodilator; steam hydrates bronchial mucosa; reduces nocturnal mucus plugging

interventions.intervention: Light easy-to-digest dinner: no dairy, no cold foods
interventions.physiological_effect: Large meals worsen nocturnal asthma (diaphragm compression); no dairy prevents mucus formation before sleep

interventions.intervention: Warm mustard oil chest and back massage
interventions.physiological_effect: Overnight mucolysis; reduces nocturnal bronchospasm via parasympathetic skin stimulation

interventions.intervention: Jala Neti (nasal irrigation)
Buteyko breathing
Pranayama
interventions.frequency: Daily (15 min — no Kapalabhati in wheeze)

interventions.intervention: Chest mustard oil pack
Steam inhalation (Vasaka)
Yoga (chest-opening)
Anti-allergic diet (dairy-free)

practices.maintenance_dose: If wheeze returns or on polluted days

relapse_rules.action: Phase 2 for 4 weeks + daily chest packs

dietary_protocol.eat_window: 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM (no large evening meal — diaphragm compression worsens nocturnal asthma)
dietary_protocol.meal_order: Anti-histamine foods first → Protein → Anti-inflammatory fats → Small complex carbs

fasting_rules.rule: No large meals within 3 hours of bedtime
fasting_rules.physiological_effect: Large meals elevate diaphragm → reduces FRC; increases GERD (acid reflux triggers asthma in 75% of patients); light early dinner reduces nocturnal bronchospasm
Vasicine peaks in bronchial mucosa 20–30 min post-dose; pre-meal timing ensures bronchodilatory cover during afternoon activity

fasting_rules.rule: Warm water throughout day — never cold
fasting_rules.specification: All water and drinks warm or at room temperature only
fasting_rules.physiological_effect: Warm water maintains bronchial mucosal hydration; cold water can trigger vagally-mediated bronchospasm in sensitive patients

fasting_rules.specification: 1 tsp honey + 1/2 inch ginger juice in warm water — on waking
fasting_rules.physiological_effect: Honey hydrogen peroxide anti-inflammatory on bronchial mucosa; ginger gingerols inhibit phosphodiesterase → bronchial smooth muscle relaxation

fasting_rules.specification: Cooked onion, apple, or capers at every meal
fasting_rules.physiological_effect: Quercetin stabilises mast cell membranes → prevents IgE-mediated histamine and leukotriene release → reduces bronchial allergic response

phase_macros.carbohydrates: Moderate: 40–45% (no processed foods; no sulphite-containing dried fruits; anti-inflammatory whole grains)
phase_macros.protein: Moderate: 20–25% (fish 3×/week; avoid shellfish and peanuts — common asthma allergens)
phase_macros.fat: Moderate: 30–35% (omega-3 dominant; no dairy fats; no trans fats)
phase_macros.rationale: Anti-inflammatory anti-histamine diet; eliminate all dairy; maximise omega-3:omega-6 ratio; no cold or refrigerated foods; no sulphite-containing foods

phase_macros.carbohydrates: Moderate: 45% (increase quercetin-rich vegetables and fruits; identify individual allergens via food diary)
phase_macros.fat: Moderate: 30% (olive oil, flaxseed oil, fish oil)
phase_macros.rationale: Allergen identification via systematic reintroduction; track peak flow diary with food diary to identify personal triggers
Lifelong dairy-free diet; allergen avoidance personalised to triggers from Phase 2; Mediterranean diet reduces asthma severity 40% (epidemiological data)

items: Quercetin: cooked onions, cooked apples, capers
Vitamin C: amla, guava (reduces histamine levels)
Ajwain (carom seeds) in cooking or steam
All vegetables (cooked, not raw in acute phase)
All cow milk and milk products

meals.what_to_eat: Jala Neti immediately; then 2 glasses warm water + 1 tsp honey + 1/2 inch ginger juice
meals.physiological_rationale: Neti clears overnight allergen load; honey-ginger: anti-inflammatory bronchial priming; warm water (never cold) prevents vagal bronchospasm

meals.what_to_eat: Buteyko breathing (10 min nasal-only) + Anulom Vilom (10 min)
meals.physiological_rationale: CO2 normalisation via Buteyko prevents hyperventilation-bronchospasm cycle; Anulom Vilom reduces mast cell reactivity

meals.what_to_eat: Oats porridge (oat milk or water — NO dairy) + cooked apple + turmeric + ginger + amla juice (30 mL)
meals.physiological_rationale: Oats quercetin + beta-glucan reduce airway inflammation; cooked apple avoids cold trigger; vitamin C reduces histamine by 38% (RCT)

meals.what_to_eat: Vasaka + Licorice decoction (1 cup, warm) — 20 min before eating
meals.physiological_rationale: Vasicine reaches peak bronchial level 20–30 min post-dose; bronchodilatory cover during afternoon peak bronchospasm risk

meals.what_to_eat: Grilled salmon (150 g) + cooked onion and garlic sabzi + brown rice (small) + cooked broccoli — no dairy, warm only
meals.physiological_rationale: Salmon omega-3 shifts LTB4 to weaker LTB5; onion quercetin mast cell stabilisation; cooked avoids cold trigger

meals.what_to_eat: Warm ginger tea + walnuts (8–10) + 1 tsp honey
meals.physiological_rationale: Ginger inhibits phosphodiesterase → sustained bronchial smooth muscle relaxation; walnuts omega-3; honey coats airway

meals.what_to_eat: Steam inhalation with ajwain (10 min) before dinner
meals.physiological_rationale: Ajwain thymol reaches bronchial epithelium via steam → bronchodilation before sleep (nocturnal asthma peak)

meals.what_to_eat: Moong dal soup (warm) + steamed vegetables (no dairy) + small ragi roti — finish by 7 PM
meals.physiological_rationale: Light early dinner prevents diaphragm compression; no dairy = no mucus formation overnight; finish 7 PM prevents GERD-triggered bronchospasm

meals.what_to_eat: Warm mustard oil chest + back massage (15 min) then sleep
meals.physiological_rationale: Mustard oil maintains overnight bronchial mucosal circulation; warmth prevents cold-triggered overnight bronchoconstriction

functional_foods.name: Vasaka (Adhatoda vasica)
functional_foods.dose_timing: 1 cup decoction (5 g leaf) — 20 min before lunch; supervised
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Vasicine alkaloid is potent bronchodilator and mucolytic — 10× bromhexine in vitro; vasicine oxidises to vasicinone (longer-acting bronchodilator); also anti-inflammatory at bronchial eosinophil level

functional_foods.name: Licorice root (Yashtimadhu)
functional_foods.dose_timing: 3–5 g root decoction — 1 cup before meals, supervised (not > 6 weeks continuous)
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Glycyrrhizin inhibits 11β-HSD → potentiates local cortisol anti-inflammatory effect in bronchial wall; also mucolytic; note: avoid in hypertension

functional_foods.name: Honey (raw, unprocessed)
functional_foods.dose_timing: 1 tsp in warm water — morning on waking; combine with ginger juice
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Hydrogen peroxide and methylglyoxal: direct anti-inflammatory on bronchial mucosa; phenolic compounds stabilise mast cells; reduces nocturnal cough comparable to dextromethorphan (trial data)

functional_foods.name: Ginger
functional_foods.dose_timing: 1-inch fresh in decoction 3×/day; add freely to cooking
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: 6-Gingerol inhibits 5-LOX and phosphodiesterase → bronchial smooth muscle relaxation; reduces eosinophil chemotaxis; documented peak flow improvement

functional_foods.name: Ajwain (carom seeds) steam
functional_foods.dose_timing: 1 tsp seeds in boiling water — inhale steam 10 min, 2×/day
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Thymol is potent bronchodilator via calcium channel antagonism; mucolytic via increased bronchial secretion hydration; antispasmodic reduces bronchospasm duration

functional_foods.name: Quercetin (from foods)
functional_foods.dose_timing: Cooked onions (1 cup/day), cooked apple, capers — daily with meals
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Stabilises mast cell membranes preventing IgE-mediated degranulation; inhibits phosphodiesterase → bronchodilation; reduces eosinophil migration into bronchial wall

functional_foods.name: Turmeric
functional_foods.dose_timing: 1 tsp in warm water or dairy-free milk — morning and evening
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: Curcumin inhibits NF-kB → reduces IL-4, IL-5, IL-13 (Th2 cytokines driving eosinophilic inflammation); stabilises mast cells; reduces IgE production

functional_foods.name: Eucalyptus (steam inhalation)
functional_foods.dose_timing: 3–5 drops eucalyptus oil in boiling water — steam inhalation 10 min morning
functional_foods.mechanism_of_action: 1,8-Cineole (eucalyptol) is mucolytic and anti-inflammatory; inhibits leukocyte activation in bronchial mucosa; documented to reduce inhaled steroid dose in mild-moderate asthma (German RCT 2003)

asanas.physiological_effect: Trains diaphragm as primary breathing muscle; increases tidal volume; reduces functional residual capacity deficiency
Opens anterior chest wall; stretches intercostals improving thoracic compliance; reverses forward-flexed asthma posture
Maximum chest expansion; drains posterior lung zones; diaphragm training via abdominal compression
Stretches bronchial tree via neck-chest expansion; stimulates thymus (immune modulation — reduces Th2 bias)
Diaphragm strengthening; thoracic spine extension improves rib cage mobility and inspiratory volume
Postural drainage of upper lung lobes; reverses gravitational mucus pooling; reduces sinus congestion
Reduces bronchial hyperreactivity; documented 12% improvement in peak flow in 4 weeks; slows respiratory rate → increases CO2
Passive chest opening; reduces residual volume; full respiratory muscle relaxation

red_flags.action: Acute severe asthma — administer rescue bronchodilator; hospital emergency immediately

red_flags.sign: Cannot complete a sentence due to breathlessness
red_flags.action: Life-threatening attack — call emergency services immediately

red_flags.sign: Rescue inhaler > 3 times in 24 h with no relief
Wheeze only on one side of chest
red_flags.action: Rule out foreign body or endobronchial tumour — urgent imaging

clinical_disclaimer: This protocol is for use under qualified BNYS clinical supervision. Patients on inhaled corticosteroids must NOT reduce medication without physician guidance. During active wheeze, skip Kapalabhati and Bhastrika. Vasaka and Licorice require clinician supervision. This document is for clinical reference only.
